name: No AI-Washing
description: Public claims about the firm's AI are substantiated and not overstated (no 'AI-washing').
rationale: IOSCO Box 4 / Table 5.4: misleading, inaccurate, or false AI claims ('AI-washing') can be the basis for enforcement. Claims must be evidence-based and substantiated before appearing in disclosures.

examples.fails: a marketing claim of advanced AI use unsupported by facts

source: IOSCO FR/02/2026 — Supervisory Toolkit for AI Use in Capital Markets (May 2026), Ch.3 Tables 5 (Disclosure) and 6 (Recordkeeping & reporting).
